stories.title: Loi anti-fast fashion : la Chine menace la France de représailles
stories.summary: China warned Wednesday it may impose retaliatory measures against France over a new law targeting ultra-fast fashion, calling the regulation discriminatory and a trade barrier. The French Parliament approved the law last month, set to take effect September 1, imposing a progressive eco-tax of up to €20 per item by 2030 on fast-fashion platforms like Shein and Temu. The law also bans advertising for these brands and mandates environmental impact warnings. China argues the measure violates WTO non-discrimination principles.

stories.title: L'Eredità Summer: Marco Liorni prova a inseguire con grande eleganza La ruota della fortuna, ma la Rai doveva svegliarsi prima
stories.summary: Marco Liorni’s L’Eredità Summer debuted on Rai 1 on July 20, 2026, drawing 2.946 million viewers and an 18.1% share, trailing La Ruota della Fortuna’s 4.466 million and 27.3%. The summer version introduces new games like Gioco delle 6 Porte and features professors Greta Zuccarello and Linda Pani alongside dancers Amedeo Monzo and Gabriele Riccio. Airing nightly at 8:35 PM, it replaces the original primetime slot with a fresh summer format.

stories.title: Morant: "Feijóo está dibujando una coalición que se parece cada vez más a esos gobiernos criminales como el de Hitler"
stories.summary: Spain’s science minister Diana Morant accused PP leader Alberto Núñez Feijóo of steering a potential coalition toward policies resembling Nazi Germany, citing rising xenophobic rhetoric and racist attacks on players like Lamine Yamal. The PP demanded an apology, calling the comparison unacceptable. Morant warned of laws targeting citizens and urged the PP to condemn hate speech. A PP government with Vox remains a possibility after the July election.

stories.title: Charli XCX recrute Vincent Cassel dans le clip de son nouveau single "Camera"
stories.summary: Charli XCX has released a new single "Camera" featuring Vincent Cassel in the music video, which was shot in black and white and directed by Aidan Zamiri. The video shows Cassel playing a scene of conflict in the desert, but the shoot is repeatedly interrupted by technical issues. Cassel eventually loses patience and exits the set, mimicking his iconic gesture from the film La Haine. The single is part of Charli XCX's upcoming album "Music, Fashion, Film", set to be released on July 24.

stories.title: EastEnders airs horrific Joel Marshall attack twist in early iPlayer release
stories.summary: Joel Marshall faces brutal revenge as teens plot his downfall in EastEnders. The Albert Square teens, led by Avani and Amy, lure Joel to a park party under false pretenses after his release from prison. The episode aired early on BBC iPlayer on July 22. Ross Marshall pleads for his son’s safety as tensions escalate.

stories.title: Africa’s most developed pharmaceutical market reviews 12 Ozempic generics as drugmakers target Novo Nordisk’s dominance
stories.summary: South Africa’s drug regulator is reviewing 12 applications for generic versions of semaglutide, the active ingredient in Novo Nordisk’s Ozempic and Wegovy. The move follows the expiry of Novo Nordisk’s main patent in March 2026, opening the door for cheaper alternatives in one of Africa’s largest pharmaceutical markets. Sun Pharma has already secured approval, with Aspen, Natco and Lupin among those preparing to enter. The shift could lower costs for diabetes and weight-loss treatments across the region.

stories.title: Alejandro Garnacho transfer news: Aston Villa agree deal with Chelsea for winger
stories.summary: Aston Villa have agreed a loan deal with Chelsea to sign winger Alejandro Garnacho until the end of the 2026-27 season, with an obligation to buy. The 22-year-old Argentina international has already completed a medical in Birmingham and pre-agreed a four-year contract at Villa Park. Chelsea expect the conditional buy clause to be triggered, valuing the deal at £43m. Villa have made four signings this summer, while Chelsea recently signed Morgan Rogers for a record £117m fee from Villa.

stories.title: Meteo MARCHE Video: previsioni aggiornate
stories.summary: Heavy rain and localized thunderstorms hit the Marche region Friday, prompting a 36-hour yellow weather alert from 2 p.m. July 11 to midnight July 12. The worst hit areas included the Pesarese highlands around Piandimeleto, Mercatale and Peglio. Brief coastal and hillside showers are expected overnight Saturday into Sunday, then clearing with stable, sunny conditions through Monday.

stories.title: La Ruota della Fortuna sbarca in prima serata: ecco quando va in onda lo speciale con Gerry Scotti
stories.summary: La Ruota della Fortuna approda in prima serata giovedì 20 novembre 2026 con uno speciale evento intitolato Campioni. Il format, condotto da Gerry Scotti, celebrerà i nove concorrenti più vincenti della stagione in una sfida d'élite. La puntata segna il passaggio del quiz dal prime time all'access prime time di Canale 5. Il programma, tra i più seguiti in Italia, raddoppia gli appuntamenti per premiare il successo di ascolti.

stories.title: INTERVIEW EXCLUSIVE - Monique Barbut, ses explications sur son revirement spectaculaire
stories.summary: Monique Barbut, ministre de la Transition écologique, had initially planned to resign Wednesday after Parliament definitively adopted an agricultural emergency law she opposed, calling it a step too far on environmental retreat. President Emmanuel Macron convinced her to stay, assuring her environmental priorities would take precedence in coming weeks. Barbut agreed to continue her role despite her disagreement with the law’s pesticide measures. Her office confirmed the decision midday on July 22.

stories.title: Restablecida la circulación de Cercanías en Madrid entre Getafe y Parla tras un incendio junto a la vía
stories.summary: A fire near train tracks in Madrid forced a 30-minute suspension of Cercanías train service between Getafe Centro and Parla on Wednesday. The disruption, which affected trains on the C4 line, was resolved by 6 pm. Trains are now gradually resuming their normal frequencies.

stories.title: Ola de calor en España: ahora llega el punto álgido con hasta 44ºC
stories.summary: Temperatures in Spain are expected to reach a peak of up to 44 degrees Celsius between Wednesday and Thursday, with the red alert already active in Murcia, Alicante, and Valencia. The hottest areas will vary by day, but the interior peninsular and the Balearic Islands are expected to see temperatures above 35 degrees Celsius. A general temperature increase is forecast for the Cantabrian region and the Ebro area, while the Mediterranean region may see a slight relief from the heat.

stories.title: Cameron Menzies became unwell during his second-round match against Ross Smith
stories.summary: Cameron Menzies' match against Ross Smith at the World Matchplay Darts was abandoned after the Scot fell ill and was taken off stage for medical attention. Menzies was 2-1 up in the second-round match when he doubled over in discomfort. The match was officially abandoned, with Smith progressing to the quarter-finals. Menzies is conscious and talking to his partner, according to medics.

stories.title: World Matchplay Darts LIVE! Updates, video, analysis, scores, commentary with Luke Littler, Michael van Gerwen, Gary Anderson in action
stories.summary: Luke Littler hit a nine-darter in the first leg of his 2026 World Matchplay second-round match against Nathan Aspinall on July 18. The PDC event runs from July 18 to July 21 at the Winter Gardens in Blackpool. Littler nearly matched the feat moments later but missed the double eight. The tournament features top players like Michael van Gerwen and Gary Anderson.

stories.title: Death of teen in crash as garda pursued ruled accidental
stories.summary: A 14-year-old boy, Thomas Healy, died in a car crash five years ago in Killarney, Co Kerry, after being pursued by a garda van with its blue lights flashing. The inquest returned a verdict of accidental death. The driver of the van, Garda Seán O'Sullivan, lost sight of the car as it rounded a bend and sped away. An investigation by the Garda Síochána Ombudsman Commission in 2022 cleared Gda O'Sullivan of any responsibility.

stories.title: World Matchplay Darts 2026 LIVE RESULTS: Play SUSPENDED during Menzies' match
stories.summary: Play at the World Matchplay Darts 2026 has been suspended due to Cameron Menzies' apparent illness, with the Scotsman leading 2-1 against Ross Smith. Menzies has been confirmed as unable to return to the match. The tournament continues with Gian van Veen and Gerwyn Price competing in the quarter-finals. The outcome of Menzies' match remains uncertain.

stories.title: What To Watch Tuesday: Kitchen Nightmares Returns, Real Housewives Of London Debuts, And More
stories.summary: Comedian Bill Maher accepts the Mark Twain Prize for American Humor on July 21, 2026. The ceremony features tributes from Louis C.K., Whitney Cummings and Woody Harrelson. The event airs on Netflix. Maher’s prize honors his five-decade career in comedy and political commentary.

stories.title: World Matchplay Darts LIVE! Updates, video, analysis, scores, commentary with Gerwyn Price, James Wade, Gian van Veen in action
stories.summary: Gerwyn Price leads the World Matchplay Darts 2026 after advancing to the quarterfinals on July 22. He defeated James Wade in a closely contested match. The event is being held at the Winter Gardens in Blackpool, England. Price's victory sets up a potential showdown with top-seeded player, but details on his next opponent have not been confirmed.

stories.title: [Galaxy Unpacked July 2026] A First Look at Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, Galaxy Z Fold8 and Galaxy Z Flip8
stories.summary: Samsung unveiled the Galaxy Z series at Galaxy Unpacked July 2026 in London, featuring three foldable devices: Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, Galaxy Z Fold8, and Galaxy Z Flip8. The Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra is the first Ultra model in the series, offering ultra-level performance and a spacious 8-inch main display. It measures 4.1 millimeters thick when unfolded. The new lineup combines AI with tailored hardware for different lifestyles.

stories.title: Price-hiked iPads are a little cheaper right now
stories.summary: Apple's high-end 13-inch iPad Pro M5 with 512GB of storage is currently discounted by $150 at Amazon and $100 at Best Buy, now priced at $1,549.99. This premium iPad features an M5 processor, OLED screen, and support for the Apple Pencil Pro and Magic Keyboard. The 11-inch iPad Air M3 with 1TB of storage is also discounted by $500, now priced at $749. These deals are available for a limited time.

stories.title: EastEnders kicks off Linda Carter romance story as new character arrives
stories.summary: EastEnders has introduced a new character, Clive Masters, played by Jason Durr, who sparks a romance with Linda Carter. The storyline begins with Linda organizing a salsa dancing lesson at the community centre, where she meets Clive, a local councillor. The pair quickly develop a connection, with Linda's friends sensing the chemistry between them. The community centre's fate hangs in the balance as Linda and Clive's relationship blossoms.

stories.title: Gespierde armen, krachtige taal: Van Persie en Steijn beloven 'heel veel goals'
stories.summary: Feyenoord's Shaqueel van Persie and Sem Steijn are back in training after recovering from knee injuries, with 32 players attending the team's training camp in Belgium. Van Persie aims to become the team's first striker, playing 90 minutes constantly and scoring many goals. The young forward is seeking to impress new trainer Giovanni van Bronckhorst, who replaced Van Persie's father, Robin, this summer. Van Persie's ambitions are clear, but he must now prove himself to the new coach.

stories.title: Hollywood’s Favorite Ugg Slippers (To Never Wear on a Christopher Nolan Set)
stories.summary: Christopher Nolan has banned Ugg slippers from his film sets, citing them as a "signifier of modernity" that can disrupt the on-screen reality. This policy was evident in his latest film, "The Odyssey," where actors wore traditional Ancient Greek attire. The ban was reportedly in place to maintain the authenticity of the setting. The film's release has sparked debate over various aspects, including representation and historical accuracy.

stories.title: Burnham stirs up political row with review of release plans for violent criminals
stories.summary: Manchester's mayor, Andy Burnham, has ordered a review of plans to release thousands of violent offenders from prison early, sparking a deepening political row. Approximately 6,000 offenders are set to be freed on tags in September. The review comes after the family of PC Andrew Harper expressed outrage over the potential release of his killers. The outcome of the review will determine the future of the early release plans.

stories.title: Sophie Thatcher Is Ready to Move Beyond ‘Yellowjackets’ and Step Out of the Darkness
stories.summary: Actress Sophie Thatcher is stepping out of the darkness following her roles in shows like "Yellowjackets" and films like "Heretic" and "Companion". She has appeared in 3 films and 1 TV series since 2020. Thatcher is now looking to move beyond her dark on-screen portrayals. Her next project is expected to showcase a different side of her acting abilities.

stories.title: iOS code could reportedly let Apple cut off apps when users miss iPhone payments
stories.summary: Apple is reportedly planning to launch a new financing program for leasing iPhones, which could allow the company to restrict access to nearly all apps on a device if payments are missed. According to code found in an iOS 27 beta, iPhones in "Restricted Mode" would lose access to most apps, except for basic functions. The feature, part of Find My, would block users from erasing or reselling the device. This new program is expected to be officially announced soon.

stories.title: Bose Wants to Be the Centerpiece of Your Living Room Again With the Lifestyle Ultra Speaker
stories.summary: Bose has launched the Lifestyle Ultra Speaker, its flagship wireless speaker, available at Best Buy for $299. The speaker is part of the reimagined Lifestyle Collection, which also includes the Lifestyle Ultra Soundbar and Subwoofer. The new speaker features three drivers and proprietary audio processing technologies. The Lifestyle Ultra Speaker is currently a bestseller on Best Buy, with an exclusive bundle available for $1,699.

stories.title: Teenager drops social media addiction lawsuit against Meta
stories.summary: A Florida teenager, 15-year-old R.K.C., has dropped his lawsuit against Meta, citing concerns about a lengthy jury trial, after previously settling similar claims against TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube. The lawsuit claimed Meta's platforms, including Instagram, were addictive and harmed children. Meta has defended itself against the lawsuit, stating the claims were baseless. R.K.C. will now focus on his recovery and therapy as he aspires to lead a normal life.

stories.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
stories.summary: A 70-year-old woman has been formally investigated in connection with the 2024 stabbing death of 65-year-old British-South African mother Karen Carter in Trémolat, France. Carter was found with stab wounds near her car by a friend in the Dordogne village where she lived. The woman was initially taken into custody in 2025 as part of preliminary inquiries before being released. A trial may now proceed if the investigating judge finds sufficient evidence.

stories.title: Villa agree loan deal for Chelsea winger Garnacho
stories.summary: Aston Villa has agreed a loan deal for Chelsea winger Alejandro Garnacho until the end of the 2026-27 season, with an obligation to buy for £43m if certain conditions are met. The 22-year-old has already completed a medical in Birmingham and pre-agreed a four-year contract at Villa Park. This deal follows Chelsea's signing of Morgan Rogers from Villa for a record £117m fee. Garnacho's departure from Stamford Bridge is expected to be permanent if the conditions are met.

stories.title: €1.5 billion for tax cuts in Budget - Economic Statement
stories.summary: The Irish government's Summer Economic Statement reveals a €1.5 billion allocation for tax cuts in the upcoming Budget, set to benefit workers through reduced personal taxation. Total expenditure is expected to rise by 5.9% to €125 billion in 2027, with €7 billion in additional spending next year. The tax package of €8.5 billion will be implemented alongside increased day-to-day spending and capital expenditure on infrastructure. The government will publish the full statement later today.

stories.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to destroy Iranian bridges and power plants if attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz continue. The US has launched strikes on Iran for the 11th consecutive night, prompting Tehran to activate air defenses in the capital. Trump's threat targets potential sites in Tehran, including those near the city. Iran has warned of reprisals after the US said it would soon hit an underground nuclear site.

stories.title: Tories ask HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage owes tax on £5m gift
stories.summary: Tories have asked HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage should have paid tax on a £5m gift from crypto billionaire Christopher Harborne. The gift was given with no expectation of return, according to Farage and Harborne's lawyers. HMRC will now determine if the payment was a taxable compensation for Farage's potential loss of earnings. The outcome of this investigation could have significant implications for Farage's financial situation.

stories.title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
stories.summary: NASA is set to unveil its first new wind tunnel in over 40 years at the Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on July 31. The Flight Dynamics Research Facility will be showcased in a media tour and ribbon-cutting ceremony. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman and center director Dr. Trina Dyal will be available for a brief media availability. The event marks a significant milestone in NASA's evolution and will likely advance the agency's research capabilities.

stories.title: Après les fortes chaleurs, des orages possiblement violents sont attendus… mais jusqu’à quand va durer la canicule dans la région
stories.summary: A prolonged heatwave continues to affect the Languedoc region, with temperatures exceeding 30 degrees Celsius expected to last until Saturday. According to Météo France, a significant change in weather conditions is anticipated on Saturday. The region can expect a mix of mistral and tramontane winds, contributing to rising temperatures. As the weather shifts, residents can expect a potential decrease in temperatures by the weekend.

stories.title: The Hawk review – Will Ferrell’s dated golf comedy just isn’t that funny
stories.summary: Will Ferrell's new Netflix show, "The Hawk," is a dated golf comedy that fails to deliver laughs. The 59-year-old actor doubles down on his signature broad comedy style, a move that contrasts with his peers who have adapted to changing tastes. The show is a throwback to Ferrell's earlier work, but it may not resonate with modern audiences. Ferrell's attempt to revive his old formula may leave viewers feeling like they're stuck in a sand trap.

stories.title: Tennis: Ana Ivanović macht ein trauriges Geständnis
stories.summary: Former tennis star Ana Ivanović has revealed she ended her career early due to mental health issues, in addition to physical injuries. The 38-year-old, who won the 2008 French Open and reached number one in the world, experienced severe panic attacks on court. Ivanović made the admission on a Croatian sports talk show, marking the first time she has publicly discussed her mental health struggles. Her early retirement in 2016 had previously been attributed to her numerous injuries.

stories.title: Eskalation in Nahost: Flugabwehr in Teheran im Einsatz – Demonstranten stören Hegseth-Auftritt im Kongress
stories.summary: Iran's air defense system has been activated in Tehran for the first time since the recent escalation in the Iran conflict, with reports of explosions and air defense fire in various districts of the city. The US military has launched a new wave of attacks on Iranian military targets, the eleventh consecutive night of strikes. The attacks aim to weaken Iran's ability to attack ships in the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial oil trade route. The consequences of this escalation remain to be seen.

stories.title: Rangers to fight McInnes ban for 'disgusting' penalty remark
stories.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has been given a four-match touchline ban by the Scottish FA for calling a late penalty decision "disgusting". The incident occurred at Motherwell in May when Celtic's Kelechi Iheanacho converted a stoppage-time spot-kick. McInnes is contesting the ban, claiming his remarks were directed at the decision, not officials. He will serve the first three games of the ban, with the fourth suspended until the end of the season.

stories.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead in Paris
stories.summary: A modelling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein, Daniel Siad, has been found dead at his home in Colombes, a north-west suburb of Paris. He was 69 years old. An investigation into his death has been opened, with a post-mortem examination to be carried out. The Paris prosecutor's office had previously investigated Siad in February for organised human trafficking and conspiracy charges, but lacked sufficient evidence to arrest him.

stories.title: « L’Agence : nouvelles destinations » est de retour sur TMC, que réserve la saison 2 ?
stories.summary: The reality TV show "L'Agence : nouvelles destinations" returns to TMC for its second season, with Martin and Valentin Kretz competing to find new destinations for their family's luxury real estate agency. The brothers, members of a family of high-end agents, aim to expand their business by discovering new markets and horizons. The competition, which drew nearly 500,000 viewers per episode in 2025, resumes on July 22, 2026. The brothers will choose their destinations and the winner will be determined.

stories.title: Fast Sylvester Stallone getötet: Dolph Lundgrens Reaktion auf den schlimmen Action-Vorfall bei „Rocky“
stories.summary: Sylvester Stallone suffered a serious injury during filming of "Rocky IV - The Fight of the Century" in 1985. He was hospitalized after being punched by co-star Dolph Lundgren. The incident occurred during a boxing scene, where Lundgren landed a real punch on Stallone. The injury was severe enough to warrant hospitalization.

stories.title: Gil Vicente-FC Porto: canta o galo para a nova temporada
stories.summary: Gil Vicente hosted FC Porto in a preseason match at Estádio Cidade de Barcelos, with the game ending in a 3-2 victory for Gil Vicente. The match was played in front of a nearly full crowd, with FC Porto fans making up the majority. This was the first home game of the 2026/27 season for Gil Vicente. The team will look to build on this momentum as they prepare for the new season.

stories.title: Inter Miami under investigation after signing Casemiro
stories.summary: Inter Miami is under investigation by Major League Soccer for alleged tampering in signing Brazil midfielder Casemiro, a 34-year-old player who left Manchester United. The investigation centers on the club's acquisition of Casemiro's discovery rights from the LA Galaxy. The MLS is reviewing the case and will release further information upon completion. The outcome may impact the terms of Casemiro's deal, which runs until 2027 with an option to extend until 2029.

stories.title: Dubai Cuts Deal For New Port to Bypass Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: A Dubai-based logistics giant unveiled a deal with the United Arab Emirates on Wednesday to develop a new port system that would bypass the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: It's Officially the End of an Era for Marvel's 'Wonder Man'
stories.summary: Marvel Studios has officially cancelled its Wonder Man Disney+ series, marking the end of an era for the character. The show was set to premiere in 2024, but production has been halted. The decision comes after a reported 12-episode order was given to the series. The future of the character remains uncertain.

stories.title: Laredo’s Future Rides on Trump’s Next Trade Move
stories.summary: The economy of Laredo, Texas, has boomed under the president’s global tariffs. Now, his demands may threaten its success.

stories.title: Israeli Banks Threaten to Cut Off Palestinian Economy
stories.summary: Two Israeli financial firms have long served as partners to Palestinian banks, handling transactions that make possible the West Bank’s imports and exports.

stories.title: Hitler’s Birthplace in Austria Is Now a Police Station
stories.summary: The government repurposed the 17th century building in hopes of preventing the site from becoming an attraction for Nazi sympathizers.

stories.title: Sony Revives Cinerama Dome in Los Angeles as Box Office Sales Rebound
stories.summary: The iconic Cinerama Dome and adjacent 14-screen theater complex will reopen in 2028 after a renovation. The venue closed in 2020 because of the pandemic.

stories.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
stories.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

stories.title: Global Oil Price Jumps as War Threats Intensify
stories.summary: But stock investors shrug off Iran developments as they await crucial earnings reports from tech firms including Alphabet and Tesla.

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health select committee report has found that obesity is an engineered outcome, not a result of individual willpower. The report recommends targeting ultra-processed foods, which are high in sugar and refined carbohydrates, as the primary drivers of weight gain and type 2 diabetes. According to the report, these foods are engineered to be highly palatable, leading to overconsumption. The committee is urging supermarkets and manufacturers to reformulate their products to reduce sugar and salt content.

stories.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
stories.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City's legionnaires' disease outbreak, with 82 cases now identified on Manhattan's Upper East Side. Eight people remain hospitalized, while 56 have been discharged. The outbreak is linked to 34 cooling towers across 33 buildings, where live legionella bacteria have been confirmed. The city's health department will continue to test the remaining 140 cooling tower systems.

stories.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
stories.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.

offbeat.title: Loi anti-fast fashion : la Chine menace la France de représailles
offbeat.summary: China warned Wednesday it may impose retaliatory measures against France over a new law targeting ultra-fast fashion, calling the regulation discriminatory and a trade barrier. The French Parliament approved the law last month, set to take effect September 1, imposing a progressive eco-tax of up to €20 per item by 2030 on fast-fashion platforms like Shein and Temu. The law also bans advertising for these brands and mandates environmental impact warnings. China argues the measure violates WTO non-discrimination principles.

The summer version introduces new games like Gioco delle 6 Porte and features professors Greta Zuccarello and Linda Pani alongside dancers Amedeo Monzo and Gabriele Riccio. Airing nightly at 8:35 PM, it replaces the original primetime slot with a fresh summer format.

A PP government with Vox remains a possibility after the July election.

The video shows Cassel playing a scene of conflict in the desert, but the shoot is repeatedly interrupted by technical issues. Cassel eventually loses patience and exits the set, mimicking his iconic gesture from the film La Haine. The single is part of Charli XCX's upcoming album "Music, Fashion, Film", set to be released on July 24.

The Albert Square teens, led by Avani and Amy, lure Joel to a park party under false pretenses after his release from prison. The episode aired early on BBC iPlayer on July 22. Ross Marshall pleads for his son’s safety as tensions escalate.

The 22-year-old Argentina international has already completed a medical in Birmingham and pre-agreed a four-year contract at Villa Park. Villa have made four signings this summer, while Chelsea recently signed Morgan Rogers for a record £117m fee from Villa.

Brief coastal and hillside showers are expected overnight Saturday into Sunday, then clearing with stable, sunny conditions through Monday.

Il format, condotto da Gerry Scotti, celebrerà i nove concorrenti più vincenti della stagione in una sfida d'élite. President Emmanuel Macron convinced her to stay, assuring her environmental priorities would take precedence in coming weeks. Barbut agreed to continue her role despite her disagreement with the law’s pesticide measures. Her office confirmed the decision midday on July 22.

offbeat.title: Restablecida la circulación de Cercanías en Madrid entre Getafe y Parla tras un incendio junto a la vía
offbeat.summary: A fire near train tracks in Madrid forced a 30-minute suspension of Cercanías train service between Getafe Centro and Parla on Wednesday. The disruption, which affected trains on the C4 line, was resolved by 6 pm. Trains are now gradually resuming their normal frequencies.

offbeat.title: Ola de calor en España: ahora llega el punto álgido con hasta 44ºC
offbeat.summary: Temperatures in Spain are expected to reach a peak of up to 44 degrees Celsius between Wednesday and Thursday, with the red alert already active in Murcia, Alicante, and Valencia. The hottest areas will vary by day, but the interior peninsular and the Balearic Islands are expected to see temperatures above 35 degrees Celsius. A general temperature increase is forecast for the Cantabrian region and the Ebro area, while the Mediterranean region may see a slight relief from the heat.

offbeat.title: Cameron Menzies became unwell during his second-round match against Ross Smith
offbeat.summary: Cameron Menzies' match against Ross Smith at the World Matchplay Darts was abandoned after the Scot fell ill and was taken off stage for medical attention. Menzies was 2-1 up in the second-round match when he doubled over in discomfort. The match was officially abandoned, with Smith progressing to the quarter-finals. Menzies is conscious and talking to his partner, according to medics.

offbeat.title: World Matchplay Darts LIVE! Updates, video, analysis, scores, commentary with Luke Littler, Michael van Gerwen, Gary Anderson in action
offbeat.summary: Luke Littler hit a nine-darter in the first leg of his 2026 World Matchplay second-round match against Nathan Aspinall on July 18. The PDC event runs from July 18 to July 21 at the Winter Gardens in Blackpool. Littler nearly matched the feat moments later but missed the double eight. The tournament features top players like Michael van Gerwen and Gary Anderson.

offbeat.title: Death of teen in crash as garda pursued ruled accidental
offbeat.summary: A 14-year-old boy, Thomas Healy, died in a car crash five years ago in Killarney, Co Kerry, after being pursued by a garda van with its blue lights flashing. The inquest returned a verdict of accidental death. The driver of the van, Garda Seán O'Sullivan, lost sight of the car as it rounded a bend and sped away. An investigation by the Garda Síochána Ombudsman Commission in 2022 cleared Gda O'Sullivan of any responsibility.

offbeat.title: World Matchplay Darts 2026 LIVE RESULTS: Play SUSPENDED during Menzies' match
offbeat.summary: Play at the World Matchplay Darts 2026 has been suspended due to Cameron Menzies' apparent illness, with the Scotsman leading 2-1 against Ross Smith. Menzies has been confirmed as unable to return to the match. The tournament continues with Gian van Veen and Gerwyn Price competing in the quarter-finals. The outcome of Menzies' match remains uncertain.

offbeat.title: What To Watch Tuesday: Kitchen Nightmares Returns, Real Housewives Of London Debuts, And More
offbeat.summary: Comedian Bill Maher accepts the Mark Twain Prize for American Humor on July 21, 2026. The ceremony features tributes from Louis C.K., Whitney Cummings and Woody Harrelson. The event airs on Netflix. Maher’s prize honors his five-decade career in comedy and political commentary.

offbeat.title: World Matchplay Darts LIVE! Updates, video, analysis, scores, commentary with Gerwyn Price, James Wade, Gian van Veen in action
offbeat.summary: Gerwyn Price leads the World Matchplay Darts 2026 after advancing to the quarterfinals on July 22. He defeated James Wade in a closely contested match. The event is being held at the Winter Gardens in Blackpool, England. Price's victory sets up a potential showdown with top-seeded player, but details on his next opponent have not been confirmed.

offbeat.title: [Galaxy Unpacked July 2026] A First Look at Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, Galaxy Z Fold8 and Galaxy Z Flip8
offbeat.summary: Samsung unveiled the Galaxy Z series at Galaxy Unpacked July 2026 in London, featuring three foldable devices: Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, Galaxy Z Fold8, and Galaxy Z Flip8. The Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra is the first Ultra model in the series, offering ultra-level performance and a spacious 8-inch main display. It measures 4.1 millimeters thick when unfolded. The new lineup combines AI with tailored hardware for different lifestyles.

offbeat.title: EastEnders kicks off Linda Carter romance story as new character arrives
offbeat.summary: EastEnders has introduced a new character, Clive Masters, played by Jason Durr, who sparks a romance with Linda Carter. The storyline begins with Linda organizing a salsa dancing lesson at the community centre, where she meets Clive, a local councillor. The pair quickly develop a connection, with Linda's friends sensing the chemistry between them. The community centre's fate hangs in the balance as Linda and Clive's relationship blossoms.

offbeat.title: Gespierde armen, krachtige taal: Van Persie en Steijn beloven 'heel veel goals'
offbeat.summary: Feyenoord's Shaqueel van Persie and Sem Steijn are back in training after recovering from knee injuries, with 32 players attending the team's training camp in Belgium. Van Persie aims to become the team's first striker, playing 90 minutes constantly and scoring many goals. The young forward is seeking to impress new trainer Giovanni van Bronckhorst, who replaced Van Persie's father, Robin, this summer. Van Persie's ambitions are clear, but he must now prove himself to the new coach.

offbeat.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
offbeat.summary: A 70-year-old woman has been formally investigated in connection with the 2024 stabbing death of 65-year-old British-South African mother Karen Carter in Trémolat, France. Carter was found with stab wounds near her car by a friend in the Dordogne village where she lived. The woman was initially taken into custody in 2025 as part of preliminary inquiries before being released. A trial may now proceed if the investigating judge finds sufficient evidence.

offbeat.title: €1.5 billion for tax cuts in Budget - Economic Statement
offbeat.summary: The Irish government's Summer Economic Statement reveals a €1.5 billion allocation for tax cuts in the upcoming Budget, set to benefit workers through reduced personal taxation. Total expenditure is expected to rise by 5.9% to €125 billion in 2027, with €7 billion in additional spending next year. The tax package of €8.5 billion will be implemented alongside increased day-to-day spending and capital expenditure on infrastructure. The government will publish the full statement later today.

offbeat.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
offbeat.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to destroy Iranian bridges and power plants if attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz continue. The US has launched strikes on Iran for the 11th consecutive night, prompting Tehran to activate air defenses in the capital. Trump's threat targets potential sites in Tehran, including those near the city. Iran has warned of reprisals after the US said it would soon hit an underground nuclear site.

offbeat.title: Après les fortes chaleurs, des orages possiblement violents sont attendus… mais jusqu’à quand va durer la canicule dans la région
offbeat.summary: A prolonged heatwave continues to affect the Languedoc region, with temperatures exceeding 30 degrees Celsius expected to last until Saturday. According to Météo France, a significant change in weather conditions is anticipated on Saturday. The region can expect a mix of mistral and tramontane winds, contributing to rising temperatures. As the weather shifts, residents can expect a potential decrease in temperatures by the weekend.

offbeat.title: The Hawk review – Will Ferrell’s dated golf comedy just isn’t that funny
offbeat.summary: Will Ferrell's new Netflix show, "The Hawk," is a dated golf comedy that fails to deliver laughs. The 59-year-old actor doubles down on his signature broad comedy style, a move that contrasts with his peers who have adapted to changing tastes. The show is a throwback to Ferrell's earlier work, but it may not resonate with modern audiences. Ferrell's attempt to revive his old formula may leave viewers feeling like they're stuck in a sand trap.

offbeat.title: Tennis: Ana Ivanović macht ein trauriges Geständnis
offbeat.summary: Former tennis star Ana Ivanović has revealed she ended her career early due to mental health issues, in addition to physical injuries. The 38-year-old, who won the 2008 French Open and reached number one in the world, experienced severe panic attacks on court. Ivanović made the admission on a Croatian sports talk show, marking the first time she has publicly discussed her mental health struggles. Her early retirement in 2016 had previously been attributed to her numerous injuries.

offbeat.title: Eskalation in Nahost: Flugabwehr in Teheran im Einsatz – Demonstranten stören Hegseth-Auftritt im Kongress
offbeat.summary: Iran's air defense system has been activated in Tehran for the first time since the recent escalation in the Iran conflict, with reports of explosions and air defense fire in various districts of the city. The US military has launched a new wave of attacks on Iranian military targets, the eleventh consecutive night of strikes. The attacks aim to weaken Iran's ability to attack ships in the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial oil trade route. The consequences of this escalation remain to be seen.

offbeat.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead in Paris
offbeat.summary: A modelling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein, Daniel Siad, has been found dead at his home in Colombes, a north-west suburb of Paris. He was 69 years old. An investigation into his death has been opened, with a post-mortem examination to be carried out. The Paris prosecutor's office had previously investigated Siad in February for organised human trafficking and conspiracy charges, but lacked sufficient evidence to arrest him.

offbeat.title: « L’Agence : nouvelles destinations » est de retour sur TMC, que réserve la saison 2 ?
offbeat.summary: The reality TV show "L'Agence : nouvelles destinations" returns to TMC for its second season, with Martin and Valentin Kretz competing to find new destinations for their family's luxury real estate agency. The brothers, members of a family of high-end agents, aim to expand their business by discovering new markets and horizons. The competition, which drew nearly 500,000 viewers per episode in 2025, resumes on July 22, 2026. The brothers will choose their destinations and the winner will be determined.

offbeat.title: Fast Sylvester Stallone getötet: Dolph Lundgrens Reaktion auf den schlimmen Action-Vorfall bei „Rocky“
offbeat.summary: Sylvester Stallone suffered a serious injury during filming of "Rocky IV - The Fight of the Century" in 1985. He was hospitalized after being punched by co-star Dolph Lundgren. The incident occurred during a boxing scene, where Lundgren landed a real punch on Stallone. The injury was severe enough to warrant hospitalization.

offbeat.title: Gil Vicente-FC Porto: canta o galo para a nova temporada
offbeat.summary: Gil Vicente hosted FC Porto in a preseason match at Estádio Cidade de Barcelos, with the game ending in a 3-2 victory for Gil Vicente. The match was played in front of a nearly full crowd, with FC Porto fans making up the majority. This was the first home game of the 2026/27 season for Gil Vicente. The team will look to build on this momentum as they prepare for the new season.

offbeat.title: It's Officially the End of an Era for Marvel's 'Wonder Man'
offbeat.summary: Marvel Studios has officially cancelled its Wonder Man Disney+ series, marking the end of an era for the character. The show was set to premiere in 2024, but production has been halted. The decision comes after a reported 12-episode order was given to the series. The future of the character remains uncertain.

tech.title: Price-hiked iPads are a little cheaper right now
tech.summary: Apple's high-end 13-inch iPad Pro M5 with 512GB of storage is currently discounted by $150 at Amazon and $100 at Best Buy, now priced at $1,549.99. This premium iPad features an M5 processor, OLED screen, and support for the Apple Pencil Pro and Magic Keyboard. The 11-inch iPad Air M3 with 1TB of storage is also discounted by $500, now priced at $749. These deals are available for a limited time.

tech.title: iOS code could reportedly let Apple cut off apps when users miss iPhone payments
tech.summary: Apple is reportedly planning to launch a new financing program for leasing iPhones, which could allow the company to restrict access to nearly all apps on a device if payments are missed. According to code found in an iOS 27 beta, iPhones in "Restricted Mode" would lose access to most apps, except for basic functions. The feature, part of Find My, would block users from erasing or reselling the device. This new program is expected to be officially announced soon.

tech.title: Teenager drops social media addiction lawsuit against Meta
tech.summary: A Florida teenager, 15-year-old R.K.C., has dropped his lawsuit against Meta, citing concerns about a lengthy jury trial, after previously settling similar claims against TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube. The lawsuit claimed Meta's platforms, including Instagram, were addictive and harmed children. Meta has defended itself against the lawsuit, stating the claims were baseless. R.K.C. will now focus on his recovery and therapy as he aspires to lead a normal life.

entertainment.title: Hollywood’s Favorite Ugg Slippers (To Never Wear on a Christopher Nolan Set)
entertainment.summary: Christopher Nolan has banned Ugg slippers from his film sets, citing them as a "signifier of modernity" that can disrupt the on-screen reality. This policy was evident in his latest film, "The Odyssey," where actors wore traditional Ancient Greek attire. The ban was reportedly in place to maintain the authenticity of the setting. The film's release has sparked debate over various aspects, including representation and historical accuracy.

entertainment.title: Sophie Thatcher Is Ready to Move Beyond ‘Yellowjackets’ and Step Out of the Darkness
entertainment.summary: Actress Sophie Thatcher is stepping out of the darkness following her roles in shows like "Yellowjackets" and films like "Heretic" and "Companion". She has appeared in 3 films and 1 TV series since 2020. Thatcher is now looking to move beyond her dark on-screen portrayals. Her next project is expected to showcase a different side of her acting abilities.

entertainment.title: Bose Wants to Be the Centerpiece of Your Living Room Again With the Lifestyle Ultra Speaker
entertainment.summary: Bose has launched the Lifestyle Ultra Speaker, its flagship wireless speaker, available at Best Buy for $299. The speaker is part of the reimagined Lifestyle Collection, which also includes the Lifestyle Ultra Soundbar and Subwoofer. The new speaker features three drivers and proprietary audio processing technologies. The Lifestyle Ultra Speaker is currently a bestseller on Best Buy, with an exclusive bundle available for $1,699.

politics.title: Burnham stirs up political row with review of release plans for violent criminals
politics.summary: Manchester's mayor, Andy Burnham, has ordered a review of plans to release thousands of violent offenders from prison early, sparking a deepening political row. Approximately 6,000 offenders are set to be freed on tags in September. The review comes after the family of PC Andrew Harper expressed outrage over the potential release of his killers. The outcome of the review will determine the future of the early release plans.

politics.title: Tories ask HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage owes tax on £5m gift
politics.summary: Tories have asked HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage should have paid tax on a £5m gift from crypto billionaire Christopher Harborne. The gift was given with no expectation of return, according to Farage and Harborne's lawyers. HMRC will now determine if the payment was a taxable compensation for Farage's potential loss of earnings. The outcome of this investigation could have significant implications for Farage's financial situation.

politics.title: Laredo’s Future Rides on Trump’s Next Trade Move
politics.summary: The economy of Laredo, Texas, has boomed under the president’s global tariffs. Now, his demands may threaten its success.

sports.title: Villa agree loan deal for Chelsea winger Garnacho
sports.summary: Aston Villa has agreed a loan deal for Chelsea winger Alejandro Garnacho until the end of the 2026-27 season, with an obligation to buy for £43m if certain conditions are met. The 22-year-old has already completed a medical in Birmingham and pre-agreed a four-year contract at Villa Park. This deal follows Chelsea's signing of Morgan Rogers from Villa for a record £117m fee. Garnacho's departure from Stamford Bridge is expected to be permanent if the conditions are met.

sports.title: Rangers to fight McInnes ban for 'disgusting' penalty remark
sports.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has been given a four-match touchline ban by the Scottish FA for calling a late penalty decision "disgusting". The incident occurred at Motherwell in May when Celtic's Kelechi Iheanacho converted a stoppage-time spot-kick. McInnes is contesting the ban, claiming his remarks were directed at the decision, not officials. He will serve the first three games of the ban, with the fourth suspended until the end of the season.

sports.title: Inter Miami under investigation after signing Casemiro
sports.summary: Inter Miami is under investigation by Major League Soccer for alleged tampering in signing Brazil midfielder Casemiro, a 34-year-old player who left Manchester United. The investigation centers on the club's acquisition of Casemiro's discovery rights from the LA Galaxy. The MLS is reviewing the case and will release further information upon completion. The outcome may impact the terms of Casemiro's deal, which runs until 2027 with an option to extend until 2029.

facts.title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
facts.summary: NASA is set to unveil its first new wind tunnel in over 40 years at the Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on July 31. The Flight Dynamics Research Facility will be showcased in a media tour and ribbon-cutting ceremony. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman and center director Dr. Trina Dyal will be available for a brief media availability. The event marks a significant milestone in NASA's evolution and will likely advance the agency's research capabilities.

breaking.title: Dubai Cuts Deal For New Port to Bypass Strait of Hormuz
breaking.summary: A Dubai-based logistics giant unveiled a deal with the United Arab Emirates on Wednesday to develop a new port system that would bypass the Strait of Hormuz.

breaking.title: Israeli Banks Threaten to Cut Off Palestinian Economy
breaking.summary: Two Israeli financial firms have long served as partners to Palestinian banks, handling transactions that make possible the West Bank’s imports and exports.

breaking.title: Hitler’s Birthplace in Austria Is Now a Police Station
breaking.summary: The government repurposed the 17th century building in hopes of preventing the site from becoming an attraction for Nazi sympathizers.

crisis.title: Sony Revives Cinerama Dome in Los Angeles as Box Office Sales Rebound
crisis.summary: The iconic Cinerama Dome and adjacent 14-screen theater complex will reopen in 2028 after a renovation. The venue closed in 2020 because of the pandemic.

crisis.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
crisis.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

crisis.title: Global Oil Price Jumps as War Threats Intensify
crisis.summary: But stock investors shrug off Iran developments as they await crucial earnings reports from tech firms including Alphabet and Tesla.

health.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
health.summary: A British health select committee report has found that obesity is an engineered outcome, not a result of individual willpower. The report recommends targeting ultra-processed foods, which are high in sugar and refined carbohydrates, as the primary drivers of weight gain and type 2 diabetes. According to the report, these foods are engineered to be highly palatable, leading to overconsumption. The committee is urging supermarkets and manufacturers to reformulate their products to reduce sugar and salt content.

health.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
health.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City's legionnaires' disease outbreak, with 82 cases now identified on Manhattan's Upper East Side. Eight people remain hospitalized, while 56 have been discharged. The outbreak is linked to 34 cooling towers across 33 buildings, where live legionella bacteria have been confirmed. The city's health department will continue to test the remaining 140 cooling tower systems.

health.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
health.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.
